short.label: 24-48 Hour Outlook
short.assessment: Heightened but stable threat environment. Proxy activity in Iraq and Yemen elevated. No indicators of imminent direct Iranian military action. IRGC readiness posture suggests precautionary positioning rather than offensive intent. Monitor Ahvaz airspace restriction for extension.
key_drivers: Houthi resumed maritime campaign — potential IRIN coordination signal
OFAC designations may trigger retaliatory cyber ops within 48 hrs
IAEA access dispute unresolved — diplomatic pressure building

medium.label: 7-Day Outlook
medium.assessment: Risk of escalation elevated over 7-day window. EU sanctions deliberations and IAEA Board of Governors meeting scheduled 2026-03-08 likely to generate Iranian response. Proxy escalation expected to continue. Direct kinetic action against US/Israeli assets remains possible if diplomatic track collapses.
key_drivers: IAEA Board of Governors meeting 2026-03-08 — Iran likely to announce enrichment escalation
EU oil sanctions vote pending — economic pressure peak
Shahroud missile range activity suggests possible test launch for signaling
Iraqi parliamentary pressure on US forces may embolden IRGC-Q operations

long.label: 30-Day Outlook
long.assessment: Strategic equilibrium likely to persist through 30-day window absent triggering event. Iran cost-benefit calculus favors proxy pressure over direct confrontation. Oman back-channel talks may produce partial nuclear deal framework, temporarily reducing tension. Key uncertainty: Israeli unilateral action against nuclear sites.
key_drivers: Oman-mediated talks — potential partial framework agreement by end of March
Israeli election cycle — coalition politics may accelerate or delay strike consideration
Iranian presidential decisions on IAEA cooperation post-Board meeting
US force posture in region (carrier presence) as deterrent signal
Economic pressure: Rial depreciation accelerating domestic political stress
